 Re: Authorization of Professional Services Agreement with Thornton, Musso, Bellemin, Inc.  
for Evaluation of Filter Media at Ed Love  
   
 
 
Request:  
Requesting authorization of professional services contract with Thornton, Musso, Bellemin , Inc. 
in the amount of $17,127.20 for the sampling and analysis of our filter media at Ed Love WTP.  
 Project Description:  
Ed Love has several water filters that contain different layers of media to remove particles from 
water before the water moves on to other treatment steps. Over time, the media loses its  
functionality and needs replacing. With this evaluation, TMB will analyze  the media to 
determine if it needs cleaning or replacing.   
 Project Schedule:  
This project will take approximately 6 weeks to complete.  
 Project Funding:  
Funded by Ed Love 60109045 -3100.

oversee the application and performance tracking.  
 Project Schedule:

 Project Funding:  
WRRF Outside Services 60109041 -3100.

  General Information  
 The Municipal Water Pollution Prevention Report (MWPP) is a required annual submission to ADEM. The MWPP summarizes the condition of the entire sanitary sewer system during the prior calendar year and is due annually by May 31st.  Each sanitary sewer system permitted by ADEM receives a point total between 0 and 783 points.  Lower point totals are preferable.   
 The Hilliard N Fletcher WRRF received a total score of 1 40 points for calendar year 202 5.

SAHD No. 2 Utility Agreement between ALDOT and the City of Tuscaloosa.

this request is to allow the contractor sufficient time to develop and implement the procedures in 
advance of this fall.  
 Project Background: 
As part of the Runway 4 -22 Extension Project, the runway will operate under a temporary displaced 
threshold condition, impacting instrument approach availability. To maintain operational capability 
during construction and upon reopening, the Airport is evaluating the use of an FAA -approved third -
party contractor to develop temporary instrument approaches. Hughes Aerospace Corporation is 
currently the only FAA -authorized air navigation service provider capable of performing this work.  
 These temporary procedures are intended to ensure continued instrument access during inclement weather and nighttime operations  prior to full FAA procedure implementation. The temporary 
procedures would sunset once the FAA -developed permanent procedures are completed and 
published.  
 Funding: 
Elevate  
 District:  
District 1

3rd party engineering firm to review the plans on the railroad’s behalf. This service agreement is the anticipated

Stillman Blvd Bridge was built in 1938 and goes over CPKC Railroad. It is located 900 feet east of Marin Luther King

This project plans to include widening of Stillman Blvd, a 10-foot shared-use path on the north side of the road, a 
6-foot sidewalk along the south side of the road, utility relocations, decorative lighting, and security cameras.

January 21, 2025  
 
 
To: Public Projects Committee  
  
From:  Laine McCool  
Office of City Engineer  
  
RE:  Patton Lake Park Project  
Project 2021.027 .001  
 
 
Request:  
The Office of City Engineer (OCE) is re questing approval of professional services contract with McGiffert and 
Associates, LLC for providing a boundary survey pf the property associated with the Patton Lake Park project.  
The amount is a not -to-exceed cost of $8,400.00.  
 
Project Description : 
Patton Lake has been a privately held lake since the late 1950’s .  As is common with lakes, upstream sediment 
filled  in the upper part of Patton Lake causing flooding issues at Woodland Hills Drive.  While the City was 
working to find a solution to the flooding, Patton  Lake dam was breached by a significant storm event in July 
2020.  The scope of the Patton Lake Park project is to convert the lake bed into a passive public park , implement 
measures to stop upstream sedimentation, and provide stormwater storage for large rain events in the stream 
channel to alleviate flooding on Woodland Hills Drive . The survey will be utilized for the land acquisition phase 
and supplemental to the design phase of the project . 
 
Project Location : 
The project is located in District 6. 
 
Funding : 
GFRFFI  (Account # 20309030 -21027 ) (Patton Lake Park )

Project Description : 
Patton Lake has been a privately held lake since the late 1950’s .  As is common with lakes, upstream sediment 
filled  in the upper part of Patton Lake causing flooding issues at Woodland Hills Drive.  While the City was 
working to find a solution to the flooding, Patton  Lake dam was breached by a significant storm event in July 
2020.  The consulting services provided by Schoel Engineering will include Preliminary and Final Design, Bid 
Phase and Basic Engineering services in addition to  services such as surve ying, testing, permitting, and 
construction -related support. These services will convert the lake bed into a passive public park , implement 
measures to stop the upstream sedimentation, and provide stormwater storage for large rain events in the 
stream channel to alleviate flooding on Woodland Hills Drive .

RESOLUTION  
 
AUTHORIZING THE OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY TO INITIATE CONDEMNATION PROCEEDINGS 
AND TAKE OTHER NECESSARY ACTIONS FOR THE ACQUISITION OF PROPERTY FOR THE KICKER 
ROAD SIDEWALK  PROJECT  
(OCA -26-0032)  
 
WHEREAS,  the City of Tuscaloosa is undertaking the Kicker Road Sidewalk Project (the 
“Project”) to enhance transportation infrastructure, improve pedestrian accessibility, and promote public safety and economic development; and  
 
WHEREAS, in order to construct and complete the Project, it is necessary for the City to 
acquire certain parcels of real property, easements, and temporary construction easements; and  
 
WHEREAS, the City has made or will make reasonable and diligent efforts to acquire the 
necessary properties through negotiation and agreement with the affected property owners; and  
 
WHEREAS, in such cases where negotiations become unsuccessful or impractical, it is 
necessary for the City to exercise its power of eminent domain to acquire such properties in a timely and lawful manner; and  
 
WHEREAS, it is in the best interest of the City to authorize the Office of the City Attorney 
to initiate condemnation proceedings and take all other actions necessary to acquire the property interests required for the completion of the Kicker Road Sidewalk  Project in such cases 
where negotiations become unsuccessful or impractical.  
 
NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the City of Tuscaloosa as follows:  
 
1. The Office of the City Attorney is hereby authorized and directed to initiate condemnation 
proceedings, pursuant to applicable law, for the acquisition of property interests required for the construction of the Kicker Road Sidewalk Project.  
 
2. The Office of the City Attorney is further authorized to take any and all other actions deemed necessary or appropriate to acquire the needed property interests, including but not limited to title searches, appraisals, offers to purchase, and filings with the court.  
 
3. This resolution shall be effective immediately upon its adoption.

  Request:  
Requesting authorization of a balancing change order with Suncoast Infrastructure for the 3rd Avenue E

This project consisted of rehabilitation of a 24” sewer main for a 3 52 LF line segment by means of cured -

Memorandum  
November 18, 2025  
 
To: Public Projects Committee   
 
From:  Josh Norris  
 Director of Construction Management  
Office of the City Engineer  
 
RE:  Shadesbrook Drive Storm Drain Repair  
OCE Project No. 2025 .034.001 
   
Request:  
The Office of City Engineer is requesting approval of a contract with Civil Worx Construction, LLC  to repair 
the storm drain at Shadesbrook Drive.  This contract will be f or $65,000  for a 30 day  period.  
 
Contract Scope:  
This proposal include s the following:  
• Mobilize equipment to the site  
• Backfill and compact fill material to rebuild failed slope  
• Inspect existing concrete pipe  
• Tie into existing pipe and extend new concrete pipe to edge of rebuilt slope  
• Install filter fabric, 2' of rip rap, and grout at pipe discharge end  
• Install topsoil over compacted fill dirt  
• Apply seed, fertilizer, and erosion control netting over topsoil  
 
 
Project Funding:  
The contract funding is from Neighborhood Drainage fund (Account #20309030 -22515)

Memorandum 
December 2, 2025 
 
To:  Projects Committee 
 
From:  Clint Bradford, PE 
Office of the City Engineer (OCE) 
 
RE:  Rice Mine Road Slope Repair (2025.036.001) 
Professional Services Agreement 
 
Request: 
The Office of the City Engineer (OCE) is requesting approval of a professional services agreement with TTL, Inc. 
for the Rice Mine Road Slope Repair  project in the amount of $18,000.00 . 
 
Scope: 
A slope failure was recently reported along the north side of Rice Mine Road, between Overlook Road 
and Indian Hills Drive. Upon inspection, OCE identified two areas of concern, both showing signs of 
recent failure. One of these areas is located approximately five feet from the edge of the roadway. 
 
The exposed soils in these areas present an increased risk of further slope instability, particularly 
under adverse weather conditions. If left unaddressed, continued erosion could lead to material loss, 
potential roadway damage, or safety hazards for the traveling public. 
 
To identify the most cost-effective and appropriate method for repairing the slope, TTL, Inc. will 
perform geotechnical engineering services as part of this agreement. Their scope of work includes a 
geotechnical exploration, laboratory testing of soil samples, engineering analyses, and preparation of 
a detailed geotechnical report. This report will evaluate subsurface conditions—such as soil 
characteristics and depth to rock—and provide site-specific repair recommendations along with an 
estimated construction cost. 
 
Funding:  Neighborhood Drainage (20309030-22515) 
 
District: 
 District 3 
 
Project Schedule: 
 Geotechnical Report – 9 Weeks

Memorandum  
December 12th, 2025  
 
To: Public Projects Committee  
 
From: Jeff Powell  
 Tuscaloosa National Airport  
 
RE:  Approval  of amendment to Work Authorization (G -08-25) for Atkins Realis to provide 
design services for the Runway 4 -22 Extension project; Total Cost: $45,859.92  
 
 
Request: 
Approval of Addendum to AtkinsRéalis  Work Authorization G- 08-25 for additional design and bid 
phase services associated with the Runway 4 -22 Extension Projec t. This amendment accounts for 
FAA- driven design changes, expanded design requirements, and additional bid alternatives that were 
not included in the original scope.  
 
Project Background: 
AtkinsRéalis is providing engineering design services for the Runway 4 -22 Extension Project under an 
existing work authorization.  
 
In July 2025, the FAA Jackson ADO issued new review comments requiring adjustments to the project . This change necessitated substantial redesign of civil and electrical components, including 
updates to approach light  layouts, grading, drainage, FAA NAVAIDs, and Runway Protection Zone 
geometry.  In addition, the project prepared  three alternate  bid-ready configurations . These 
alternatives were incorporated to support value engineering and strengthen bid competitiveness by allowing contractors to price differing levels of scope options.  
 These FAA -directed changes and expanded bid requirements exceeded the original design scope, 
necessitating this addendum  to the AtkinsRéalis Work Authorization.   These additional costs are 
reimbursable within the existing FAA grant.  The total cost for this amendment is $45,859.92, with a 
local match obligation of  $1,146.50.   
 Funding : 
FAA/ALDOT/Elevate  
 District:  
 District 1

Memorandum  
December 12th, 2025  
 
To: Public Projects Committee  
 
From: Jeff Powell  
 Tuscaloosa National Airport  
 
RE:  Approval of Work Authorization (G -09-25) for Atkins Realis to provide construction 
administration services for the Runway 4 -22 Extension project . Total : $1,274 ,334.94  
 
 
Request:  
Approval of Work Authorization G -09-25 with AtkinsRéalis for Construction Administration, Resident Project 
Representative (RPR), and Quality Assurance (QA) materials testing services  associated with the Runway 4 -22 
Extension Project.  
 Project Background:  
AtkinsRéalis has completed design services for the Runway 4- 22 Extension Project under a prior work 
authorization. As the project transitions to construction, FAA grant requirements include  professional 
construction -phase support, including contract administration, on- site observation, materials testing, and 
project documentation to ensure compliance with FAA specifications. 
 
Work Authorization G -09-25 assigns AtkinsRéalis responsibility for the construction administration . This scope 
includes preparation of construction documents, review of contractor submittals, interpretation of contract requirements, pay application review, and preparation of record drawings. It also includes dedicated RPR 
staffing to monitor day -to-day progress, observe testing and inspec tions, and maintain project records.  
Construction -phase services will be performed over an anticipated 495- day period, beginning with 
preconstruction activities in March 2026.  
 The total Work Authorization amount is $1,274,334.94 and is eligible for reimbursement under the existing FAA 
AIP Grant supporting this p roject. This amount is a not -to-exceed value pending the completion of ALDOT ’s fee 
audit review. The local match obligation for this authorization is $31,858.37 and is already included within the current grant budget.  
 
Funding : 
FAA/ALDOT/Elevate

 District 1

Premises.  
 
Project Background: 
The Airport has  worked with Ascension FBO, LLC to identify and remove a 1.25 -acre portion of 
property —identified in the lease as “Exhibit A, Parcel 2” —from the existing Leased Premises. This 
parcel was originally added under the former Bama Air FBO lease to support an Air Medevac 
operation  in 2013, which ceased operations in 2018. The parcel has remained unused since that time 
and no longer serves an operational purpose within the FBO leasehold.  
 Releasing Parcel 2 will provide the Airport greater flexibility to promote and market future development opportunities on the east side of the airfield. Ascension has agreed to the removal of this parcel, and the amendment proportionately adjusts the rental amounts to reflect the reduced 
leasehold.

Request: 
 
Authorization is requested to award the construction contract for the Runway 4/22 and Taxiway Extensions Project to Dominion Construction Co , Inc.  in the amount of $18,852,848.40.  
 Project Background: 
 This project is funded through a grant partnership between the Federal Aviation Administration 
(95%), the Alabama Department of Transportation (2.5%), and the City of Tuscaloosa (2.5%). The 
improvements were identified in the Airport’s FAA -approved Master Plan and are a critical 
component of it s long -term airfield development program.  
 
The project was publicly advertised on September 4, 2025, with bids opened on October 23, 2025. A total of five bids were received from qualified general contractors. Following review, AtkinsRéalis certified Dominion Construction Co. as the lowest responsive and responsible bidder, confirming that the company meets all contract requirements and possesses the necessary qualifications to perform the work.  
 The Runway 4/22 and Taxiway Extensions Project will extend the existing runway and associated 
taxiways to improve airfield geometry, operational safety, and capacity for a wider range of aircraft. 
The project also includes upgrades to the airfield electrical vault, relocation of FAA navigational equipment, and installation of casing pipe to support future sewer expansion .

Contract Amendment No. 2  
 
Request: 
The Office of the City Engineer (OCE) is seeking approval of Contract Amendment No. 2 with TTL, Inc. for the 
University Blvd E Roadway Improvements Project; Additional $35,025. 
 
Background for the Request: 
This amendment adds bid phase services and reallocates funds from services that were either completed or 
deemed unnecessary to those requiring additional design and consulting. The most significant additional 
services include multiple redesigns related to the following: The northern shared-use path, utility duct banks, 
landscaping, Right-Of-Way (ROW) and easement negotiations involving affected properties. Additional services 
also including detailed coordination with Spire, addressing further ALDOT comments, and resubmitting 
Environmental Assessment (EA) documents to ALDOT. 
 
Scope: 
The University Blvd East Roadway Improvements Project is located between 30th Avenue East and 
Crescent Ridge Road at an approximate length of 3,450 feet. This project is planned to include 
construction of a 10’ shared use path on the north side of the road and 6’ sidewalk on the south side 
of the road, new storm drainage infrastructure, improvements to existing water and sewer 
infrastructure, decorative lighting, security cameras, landscaping, and the conversion of overhead 
utilities to underground, less the transmission power line.

Request: 
Approval  of Addendums to the AtkinsRéalis Work Authorization to support completion of design and 
bid advertisement services for  Bid Package #2 and to provide construction administration services for 
Bid Package #1.  
 
Project Background: 
The original Work Authorization with AtkinsRéalis was awarded in September 2023 to provide design and bid preparation services. Bid selection  of Bid Package #1 occurred June 2025  and grant award 
was secured in September 2025. Construction for this first package is scheduled to begin in October 
2025.  
 
• Addendum #2 (net $0) reallocates unspent funds from the Not -to-Exceed Cultural Resources 
Assessment Survey  (Addendum #1) into the Lump Sum Design  portion of the contract. This will 
provide the necessary budget to complete design services for Bid Package #2.  
 
• Addendum #3 authorizes Construction Administration services for Bid Package #1 ; Total cost, 
$168,826.25  
 
Looking ahead, this project is part of a multi- phase capital improvement plan funded through the 
Bipartisan Infrastructure Law (BIL), which provides airports with annual entitlement funding over a five-year period. Bid Package #2  will complete the apron construction  which  is scheduled for FY 2026 
following receipt of the final BIL funding installment.

RE:  Approval  of a Minor Public Works Contract with Stripes Painting Company  
 
 
Request:  
Approval is requested to enter into  a minor public works contract with Stripes Painting 
Company in the amount of $1,704. The scope of work includes the renewal of approximately 1,905 linear feet of airfield markings to maintain the visibility and effectiveness of required visual aids for pilots.  
 Project Background:  
Regular renewal of airfield markings is essential for maintaining operational safety and compliance with FAA standards. Due to weather exposure, aircraft traffic, and routine airfield operations, painted surface markings typically require renewal every 2 –3 years to 
preserve adequate visibility and reflectivity for pilots.  
 Stripes Painting Company has submitted a quote of $1,704 to perform the planned work. The project scope covers restriping of existing markings only, with a total estimated length of 1,905 linear feet  
 
Funding : 
Airport General Fund
